name: Almost Pasta Primavera
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the spaghetti squash and cut it in half lengthwise, discarding the seeds. Place the squash cut side down in a Dutch oven with about 2 inches of water. Bring to a boil, cover, and cook for 20 minutes or until tender.
2. Drain the squash and let cool. Using a fork, remove the spaghetti-like strands from the squash. Measure out 3 cups of these strands; set aside. The remaining strands can be used later.
3. Steam the vegetables (broccoli, zucchini, mushrooms, and carrots) for 5 to 7 minutes or until crisp-tender. Drain well.
4. Combine the cooked spaghetti squash strands and steamed vegetables in a bowl, tossing gently. Cover to keep warm; set aside.
5. In a small saucepan, melt margarine over low heat. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
6. Remove from heat and stir in skim milk, ricotta cheese, butter flavoring, salt, Italian seasoning, and ground pepper. Cook for a few seconds to warm the mixture, being careful not to boil it.
7. Spoon the cheese sauce over the vegetable mixture and toss gently.
Food Exchanges per serving: 1 food exchange + some free vegetables, 1/2 high-fat meat + 1/2 fat exchanges....
Yields 6 servings (about 77 calories, 4.5 grams protein, 2.5 grams fat, 10.3 grams carbohydrate, 7 milligrams cholesterol, 171 milligrams sodium, and 331 milligrams potassium per serving).
